avalancheActivity.highlights: At intermediate and high altitudes a critical avalanche situation will be encountered over a wide area. New snow and wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a strong southwesterly wind, avalanche prone wind slabs formed. They can be released easily and reach very large size. This applies in particular on steep slopes above the tree line, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. In many cases avalanches can be triggered in the various wind slab layers.
The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility.
Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and natural avalanches are a clear indication of a weakly bonded snowpack.
It is inadvisable to engage in backcountry touring outside marked and open pistes.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 30 to 40 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell yesterday. As a consequence of the strong southwesterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed.
Over a wide area new snow and wind slabs are lying on a weakly bonded old snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: In these regions the avalanche danger is high (level 4).
avalancheActivity.comment:
Heavy snowfall. The wind will be moderate to strong.
The snowpack remains in most cases unstable. Natural avalanches are possible over a wide area. Avalanches can penetrate deep layers reach very large size.
Without question, it is inadvisable to engage in backcountry touring.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Somewhat older wind slabs remain for the foreseeable future prone to triggering. As a consequence of new snow and wind, further wind slabs will form.
Numerous weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

bulletin.highlights: New snow is lying on surface hoar.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The new snow must be evaluated with care and prudence in all aspects above approximately 1000 m.
avalancheActivity.comment: Over a wide area in some regions up to 40 cm of snow has fallen above approximately 1500 m. The new snow can be released easily or naturally in all aspects. In particular in the regions exposed to heavier precipitation medium-sized dry avalanches are to be expected as the snowfall becomes more intense. The off-piste conditions are dangerous. Temporary safety measures may be necessary in some localities.

snowpackStructure.comment: The new snow is lying on surface hoar above the tree line. Snow profiles and stability tests have confirmed the unfavourable bonding of the snowpack. Weak layers deep in the old snowpack are difficult to recognise. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers very easily.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Considerable avalanche danger will prevail. The new snow and wind slabs represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and a moderate wind, sometimes large wind slabs formed. Wind slabs can be released, even by a single winter sport participant. This applies in particular on steep slopes, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain also at intermediate altitudes.
The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and natural avalanches are a clear indication of a weakly bonded snowpack.
Avalanches can be released in deeper layers in particular on steep shady slopes.
Careful route selection and spacing between individuals are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area 15 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell yesterday. As a consequence of new snow and a moderate to strong wind from southerly directions, precarious wind slabs formed in particular above the tree line. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on soft layers in particular on wind-protected shady slopes.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on very steep shady slopes.

bulletin.highlights: New snow, wind slabs and old snow require caution. The conditions are precarious for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Slab avalanches and loose snow slides are the main danger. Outside marked and open pistes a dangerous avalanche situation will prevail.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The fresh snow of yesterday and the wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack above the tree line. In particular on wind-loaded slopes avalanches can be released easily and reach large size in isolated cases. Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and natural avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.
Distinct weak layers in the lower part of the snowpack can be released even now easily by individual winter sport participants in particular on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies in particular above the tree line. The avalanche prone locations are numerous and are barely recognisable, even to the trained eye. The number and size of avalanche prone locations will increase with altitude.
Caution and restraint are required.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow and wind slabs of Tuesday are lying on surface hoar above the tree line.
Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies especially on west, north and east facing slopes. During the day: Over a wide area 5 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall above approximately 1200 m.

tendency.highlights: Night: In some localities up to 15 cm of snow will fall above approximately 1200 m. The avalanche danger will increase but remain within the current danger level.

avalancheActivity.highlights: At intermediate and high altitudes a considerable avalanche danger will prevail.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and wind from southerly directions, avalanche prone wind slabs formed. This applies in particular on steep slopes, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain also at intermediate altitudes. The fresh wind slabs can be released by small loads.
The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility.
Avalanches can be released in deeper layers in particular on steep shady slopes.
Maintaining distances between individuals and one-at-a-time descents are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Up to 10 cm of snow fell yesterday. As a consequence of new snow and a moderate wind, further wind slabs formed on Tuesday. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on soft layers in particular on wind-protected shady slopes.

Intermediate and high altitudes: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on very steep shady slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights:
Considerable avalanche danger will prevail.
As a consequence of new snow and wind the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase as the day progresses.
avalancheActivity.comment: The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. The avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack. They can in many places be released, even by a single winter sport participant. Shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and whumpfing sounds can indicate the danger. Great caution and restraint are required.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The snowpack remains unstable over a wide area. As a consequence of new snow and wind, further wind slabs will form.
Numerous weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The avalanche prone locations are to be found especially on steep shady slopes above the tree line. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint.
avalancheActivity.comment: In some regions in some localities up to 10 cm of snow will fall on Wednesday. Avalanches can be released in near-ground layers by small loads. Remotely triggered and natural avalanches are possible. In addition the fresh and older wind slabs must be taken into account.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The new snow of last week is lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack on shady slopes above the tree line. Towards its base, the snowpack is faceted and weak. Avalanches can be released in deeper layers very easily.
In addition the large surface-area wind slabs adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls are capable of being triggered.

region.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: Monday: In particular above approximately 2500 m: During the night the wind was moderate to strong at times. At intermediate and high altitudes a considerable avalanche danger will prevail.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of new snow and wind from southerly directions, avalanche prone wind slabs formed during the course of the night.
Single winter sport participants can release avalanches, including medium-sized ones. Mostly they are shallow. The avalanche prone locations are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility, caution is to be exercised in particular in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain, and on very steep slopes.
The fresh snow and especially the wind-blown snow drifts, both soft and hard, cover a thin layer of old snow. On very steep shady slopes the avalanches can be triggered in deep layers of the snowpack and reach large size in isolated cases. Slopes that have been little used this winter thus far where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially unfavourable.
Careful route selection and spacing between individuals are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Tuesday: 5 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen above approximately 2000 m. The new snow and wind slabs are lying on soft layers in particular on wind-protected shady slopes.
Faceted weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on west, north and east facing slopes.
Several medium-sized slab avalanches have been released in the last few days. On Friday on steep slopes small and medium-sized avalanches occurred naturally.

tendency.highlights: Some snow will fall. The meteorological conditions will prevent a change towards better conditions. The danger will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: A treacherous avalanche situation will persist. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow represent the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Distinct weak layers in the lower part of the snowpack can be released even now easily by individual winter sport participants in particular on west, north and east facing slopes. This applies in particular above the tree line, in isolated cases also in areas close to the tree line, and on steep sunny slopes at elevated altitudes. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible.
The fresh wind slabs can be released easily. or in isolated cases naturally, in particular on west to north to east facing aspects above the tree line. They can release the weakly bonded old snow as well and reach medium size.

Whumpfing sounds and the formation of shooting cracks when stepping on the snowpack and fresh avalanches serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Caution and restraint are recommended.

snowpackStructure.comment:
In particular from the Ortler Range via the Stubai Alps to the Venediger Range up to 10 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen. As a consequence of the sometimes strong wind the wind slabs will increase in size additionally.
The new snow and wind slabs are lying on top of a weakly bonded old snowpack. Distinct weak layers exist in the old snowpack. This applies especially on west, north and east facing slopes.

tendency.highlights: In particular in the east up to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall. Weak layers in the old snowpack can still be released by individual winter sport participants. In addition the fresh wind slabs are prone to triggering.

avalancheActivity.comment:
Avalanches can in isolated cases be released, but they will be small in most cases. This applies especially on very steep shady slopes, as well as adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls above the tree line.

Apart from the danger of being buried, restraint should be exercised in particular in view of the danger of avalanches sweeping people along and giving rise to falls.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The rather small wind slabs of the last few days are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack at elevated altitudes. The old snowpack consists of faceted crystals.

The snowpack will be generally subject to considerable local variations.

tendency.highlights: From a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying. Moderate avalanche danger will prevail.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The avalanche prone locations are to be found especially on steep shady slopes above the tree line. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint.
avalancheActivity.comment:
In some regions in some localities up to 10 cm of snow will fall on Wednesday.
Avalanches can be released in near-ground layers by small loads. In addition the fresh and older wind slabs must be taken into account.
In the regions exposed to heavier precipitation avalanches are possible, even medium-sized ones.

snowpackStructure.comment: In some cases new snow and wind slabs are lying on old snow containing large grains. The avalanche-prone wind slabs are lying on weak layers in particular on wind-protected shady slopes above approximately 1500 m. Avalanches can be released by small loads.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Moist and wet avalanches are the main danger.
avalancheActivity.comment: In particular in gullies and bowls and on steep slopes small and medium-sized moist slab avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment: Some new snow to above approximately 1500 m. In isolated cases new snow is lying on the soft surface of an old snowpack.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet snow represents the main danger.
In very isolated cases the avalanches in these regions are small and can be released by large loads.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Mostly the avalanches are small and can only be released by large loads.
Gliding avalanches can also occur.

snowpackStructure.comment: Areas with glide cracks are to be avoided. Below approximately 1000 m a little snow is lying.
